pi_items.source: Installation script downloads from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/smtp.pcap (official Wireshark sample captures); verified file path matches task requirements
Path hardcoded in setup_task.sh (line 18) and export_result.sh (line 28); task description confirms this as export destination

pi_items.verified_value: Infected_Client (task-defined arbitrary label; actual client IP: 10.10.1.4)
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies: 'map the Client IP to Infected_Client' (task.json line 19); metadata matches task description; actual client IP verified via tshark analysis of smtp.pcap (source IP of EHLO command)

pi_items.verified_value: Corporate_Mail_Server (task-defined arbitrary label; actual server IP: 74.53.140.153; real hostname: xc90.websitewelcome.com running Exim 4.69)
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies: 'map the Server IP to Corporate_Mail_Server' (task.json line 20); metadata matches task description; actual server IP verified via tshark analysis (source IP of SMTP 220 response: 74.53.140.153)

privileged_info_summary: Task uses official Wireshark sample SMTP capture (smtp.pcap, 60 packets, 27 KB) containing real 2009 SMTP traffic from client 10.10.1.4 to server 74.53.140.153 (xc90.websitewelcome.com running Exim 4.69). Task metadata correctly specifies the export path (/home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp_resolved.txt) and task-specific host labels (Client: Infected_Client, Server: Corporate_Mail_Server) that agents must map to the extracted IPs via Wireshark's hosts configuration file.
